DIVERGE region data: first byte differs at offset 0xbf39 (guest 0x7ebf39) -- 93 vs 94

Classification: PS3-wide, non-semantic -- HLE binding table layout.

Guest address 0x7EBF39 falls inside the HLE OPD (function descriptor)
stub area. SSHD's first HLE OPD is at 0x7EBF38 (cellResc, first
import). The diverging byte is the second byte of the first OPD slot,
which encodes part of the trampoline body address.

CellGov and RPCS3 assign HLE stub slot indices in the same module
order but may differ by one index position within a module due to
NID-table iteration order (sorted vs declaration-order). This
produces a one-byte difference in the OPD pointer field.

This is the same class of divergence seen in flOw's cross-runner
compare (data:0x7768, also an HLE binding table layout difference).
Both are PS3-wide, non-semantic: the pointed-to code is functionally
identical, only the slot-index assignment convention differs.

Code region: byte-identical (0x7c4f68 bytes match).
Data region: first divergence at offset 0xbf39 of 0x49c98 bytes.

Checkpoint boundary: CellGov captures at FirstRsxWrite (put-pointer
update to 0xC0000040); RPCS3 captures at sys_tty_write call #10.
The boundaries are close but not identical -- CellGov reaches the
RSX control register write while RPCS3 dumps at the last TTY write
before that point.
